pi_items.source: Multiple college course catalogs and OER Commons confirm BIO201 standard course name is 'Human Anatomy & Physiology I' or 'Human Anatomy and Physiology I'. The metadata shorthand 'Anatomy & Physiology' matches the common abbreviated form.

Verified via Pikes Peak, Mesa Community College, Pima Community College, Berea College, and Calhoun Community College catalogs. BIO201 is the standard course code for Human Anatomy & Physiology I across US community colleges and universities.

Northern Virginia Community College official course listing shows BIO101 is officially titled 'General Biology I'. NVCC catalog explicitly states 'BIO 101 - General Biology I' as the full course name.
Verified via Northern Virginia Community College, Virginia VCCS system, and multiple other institutions. BIO101 is the standard code for introductory general biology courses.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic test data (courses created by setup_task.sh via MariaDB). The metadata course names and codes are accurate: BIO201 is the standard code for 'Human Anatomy & Physiology I' courses across US institutions, and BIO101 is the standard code for 'General Biology I'. The prerequisite relationship (Biology I → Anatomy & Physiology) is realistic and commonly required or recommended at many colleges. The OpenSIS system supports course prerequisite management through course_reqs database table.
